prose:
Formaldehyde frontier-orbital gap — DFT, tier ‘standard’ (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz (4 atoms); single-point only (no further optimisation). SCF converged in 8 cycles.

Results (Koopmans-style orbital energies):
  HOMO  = –7.6523067 eV
  LUMO  = –1.7826355 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV

No engine warnings were reported; integrity check status: ok (trustworthy = true). Calculation ran via chemkit skill ‘frontier-orbitals’ with command:
  cli … frontier --method dft --tier standard formaldehyde.xyz --out <results>.json
A live engine log was written during execution (frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out; same directory as the run) and the structured result JSON to <results>.json for full reproducibility.

Method provenance: property (orbital energies) at B3LYP/def2-TZVP // formaldehyde.xyz; gas-phase implicit (no solvent), charge 0, multiplicity 1, PySCF 2.x with RIJK density fitting.
